description: India's T20 World Cup journey begins with a clash against Pakistan in Birmingham. Despite a strong historical record against their rivals, the Indian squad, captained by Harmanpreet Kaur, is emphasizing focus. Having had better preparation and recent wins, they know they can't underestimate Pakistan, even if the opposition isn't as ready.

title: 'The presser can wait': Nitish Reddy pauses interaction to answer mom's call - Watch
description: India's victory over Afghanistan was marked by a heartwarming moment as Nitish Kumar Reddy paused his post-match press conference for a call from his mother. The young all-rounder's touching gesture resonated widely. On the field, debutants shone with the ball, while Shubman Gill's unbeaten 84 anchored India's chase to a seven-wicket win.

Yogi’s UP beats national violent-crime decline across 5 key categories
description: Uttar Pradesh shows a significant decline in key crime indicators like rioting and kidnapping between 2022-2024, outperforming national trends. While murder and attempt to murder also saw positive shifts, rape figures reveal a complex reality where offenders are often known to victims. This data provides the Yogi government with political leverage, though public perception of safety remains a crucial battleground.

title: Aldon Smith cause of death: What happened to former All-Pro linebacker
description: Aldon Smith, the former San Francisco 49ers outside linebacker and one of the most explosive pass rushers of his generation, has died at the age of 36. The 49ers confirmed his passing on June 13, 2026, describing it as sudden and tragic. No cause of death has been made public. Smith, a first-team All-Pro in 2012 with 19.5 sacks that season, saw a career full of promise derailed by repeated off-field issues, including arrests and NFL suspensions. He last played for the Dallas Cowboys in 2020.

title: 'You're getting discombobulated': 'Commander in Chief' Trump shares AI war image
description: President Donald Trump shared an AI-generated military image on Truth Social, claiming a major breakthrough with Iran is imminent and a peace deal to prevent nuclear weapons will be signed Sunday. However, Iran's Foreign Ministry spokesperson stated an agreement is unlikely to be signed immediately, suggesting further negotiations are needed.

title: Inside Track: Pricey Presidential Perks
Late-night ride in client’s Mercedes turns fatal for car dealer, friend in Bengaluru
description: A late-night drive in a Mercedes-Benz SUV tragically ended near Hoskote after crashing into a divider on the Satellite Town Ring Road. The driver, car dealer Rizwan, was found in a water body, while his companion, Saajiya, died at the scene. Police suspect speeding and reckless driving caused the fatal accident.

Seat ejection, mud embankment may have saved lone survivor of AI 171 crash
description: Air India Flight 171 crashed shortly after takeoff from Ahmedabad on June 12, 2025, killing 241 people. Miraculously, one passenger, Viswashkumar Ramesh, survived the devastating incident. An internal police assessment revealed his survival was due to a unique combination of factors, including his seat's position and a protective mud embankment.

UP govt forms 3-member SIT to investigate Ram temple donation case
description: Uttar Pradesh government has formed a three-member SIT to investigate alleged embezzlement of donations at the Ram Temple in Ayodhya. The move follows a request from the Shri Ram Janmabhoomi Teerth Kshetra Trust, which seeks to counter misinformation. The SIT will submit a preliminary report within seven days.

description: Uttar Pradesh Chief Minister Yogi Adityanath strongly condemned derogatory social media comments targeting Akhilesh Yadav's daughter, deeming such remarks against any daughter unacceptable. He directed police action and urged the Samajwadi Party chief to ensure his workers also exercise restraint in their language, emphasizing the need for civility in public discourse.

Ladies’ first: Maiden batch of 9 from IMA now officers
description: A historic moment unfolded at the Indian Military Academy as nine women officer cadets were commissioned for the first time, joining 515 graduating cadets. President Droupadi Murmu hailed the event as a significant step for women's empowerment and fostering international ties. Awards were presented to outstanding cadets, including Vishal Kumar for 'Best Overall Cadet'.

description: The US government has restricted global access to Anthropic's advanced AI models, Fable 5 and Mythos 5, citing national security concerns. This move, stemming from fears of AI misuse for cyberattacks, signals a shift towards treating frontier AI as a strategic asset, impacting international relations and AI development.

Reborn in the USA: Folarin Balogun and the joy of the global football
description: Folarin Balogun, scorer of two goals on his World Cup debut, was born in Brooklyn to Nigerian parents after his pregnant mother, visiting her sister in America, was reportedly advised against flying back to Britain, where she lived at that time. In the more extreme corners of the MAGA universe, that would make Balogun an "anchor baby" – born to someone visiting the US. On Thursday night, he was simply called "Man of the Match" the undisputed hero of a squad that perfectly represents the beautiful, messy, borderless reality of modern soccer.

Canada's sixth-largest state Manitoba retires student pathway to permanent residency
description: Manitoba has closed its Career Employment Pathway for international graduates, effective immediately. Candidates with active EOIs and six months of provincial work experience can now be considered under the Skilled Worker in Manitoba Pathway. This change aims to align graduates' skills with labor market needs, prioritizing those who studied and are working in the province.
